vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Structural integrity for wing spars
Cooling path for motor pylon
Equipment bay door access
Service access cutout
Mounting datum for motor pod

risk.desc: Potential structural weakness at spar attachment points due to EPP foam core.
risk.mit: Use additional CFRP reinforcement plates at attachment points.

risk.desc: Cooling inlet may not provide sufficient airflow if improperly positioned.
risk.mit: Include airflow simulation during design phase to validate positioning.

risk.desc: Service access cutout might obstruct internal components if too small.
risk.mit: Review component sizes and adjust cutout dimensions accordingly.

risk.desc: Fastener joint integrity could be compromised if not properly torqued.
risk.mit: Implement torque verification step during assembly process.

risk.desc: Threaded connections into printed parts may strip if not using proper inserts.
risk.mit: Ensure all threaded connections use appropriate inserts or bosses.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

physics_paths.description: Structural loads from wing spars transferred to pod shell
Cooling air flows through inlet to motor pylon area
Thermal management via pod shell material properties
Opening and closing motion of equipment bay door
Human access for maintenance and equipment installation

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structural integrity under aerodynamic loads
Continuous load path from wing skin to fuselage frames
Anti-rotation functionality
Service access for maintenance
Integration with adjacent subsystems

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence for critical joints
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion mismatch between CFRP spar and EPP foam core
risk.mit: Conduct thermal cycling tests to validate material compatibility

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex interface requirements
risk.mit: Implement detailed assembly instructions and training for operators

risk.desc: Possibility of insufficient stiffness in the carry-through section under high loads
risk.mit: Perform finite element analysis to confirm structural integrity

risk.desc: Risk of improper fastener installation leading to preload loss over time
risk.mit: Specify torque values and conduct regular maintenance checks

risk.desc: Potential for water ingress through service access holes if not properly sealed
risk.mit: Design and install appropriate seals and gaskets around access points

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Transfers lift and drag forces from wing skin to main spar
Heat dissipation from sun exposure through CFRP spar
Transmits engine thrust to fuselage frames via carry-through

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Integrate anti-rotation key with wing skin for secure fit
integration_opportunities.description: Anti-rotation key should align with corresponding feature on wing skin

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Optimize rib spacing for uniform load distribution
integration_opportunities.description: Ribs should be evenly spaced along the spar to ensure structural integrity

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Ensure smooth transition from spar to carry-through
integration_opportunities.description: Smooth transition between spar and carry-through to maintain load continuity

manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented for easy removal
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection and CMM for critical dimensions

verification_focus.description: Verify total mass does not exceed 1500g
Ensure continuous load path from wing skin to fuselage frames
Confirm anti-rotation key prevents unwanted rotation during flight

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to bolted connections.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels
physics_paths: Load path: wing spar loads transferred to servo pocket via control horn
Flow path: air flows over wing surface, affecting control horn and pushrod
Thermal path: heat generated by servo transferred to wing structure via contact
Motion path: control horn moves in response to servo actuation, altering wing surface angle
Pressure path: aerodynamic pressure acts on wing surface, transmitted to control horn

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and tactile inspection for alignment and clearance
